question: Consider the three-dimensional Ising model on a simple cubic lattice with quenched nonmagnetic impurities arranged as mutually parallel linear defects running along the z-axis (impurity concentration p=0.2, meaning 20% of sites along each z-column are nonmagnetic). The system exhibits anisotropic critical scaling with distinct perpendicular (perp, in the xy-plane) and parallel (||, along z) correlation-length exponents: xi_perp ~ |t|^{-nu_perp} and xi_|| ~ |t|^{-nu_||}, where t = (T-T_c)/T_c. The anisotropy ratio theta = nu_|| / nu_perp satisfies 1.1 <= theta <= 1.3 as established by Binder-cumulant and correlation-length scaling. An anisotropic FSS study uses L_|| = L_perp^theta (rounded to nearest integer) for perpendicular sizes L_perp = 10..100, and 128 disorder realizations per size. The susceptibility chi = beta V (overline{<M^2> - <|M|>^2}) is fitted as chi ~ L_perp^b at the pseudocritical inverse temperature beta_max^A = 0.25655(10). Report the exponent ratio as a JSON object with key: "gamma_over_nu_perp" (ratio gamma/nu_perp, where gamma is the susceptibility exponent and nu_perp is the perpendicular correlation-length exponent). Use the value averaged over the theta range [1.1, 1.3]. Provide a float.

metadata.system: 3D Ising model, simple cubic, parallel linear defects along z, impurity concentration p=0.2
metadata.source_excerpt: using anisotropic FSS fits of the susceptibility at beta_max^A=0.25655(10) with L_||=L_perp^theta for 1.1<=theta<=1.3, the fitted exponent b yields gamma/nu_perp ~= 1.85 +/- 0.05 (averaged over theta window). For theta=1.1 (favored by Binder analysis): gamma/nu_perp ~= 1.90 +/- 0.08.
metadata.difficulty_note: Anisotropic FSS; result depends on choice of theta. Only gamma/nu_perp extracted (not nu_perp separately). Compare to RG estimates ~2.0.
